differential_diagnoses: Euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis (secondary to SGLT2 inhibitor use)
Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinemia secondary to chronic acetaminophen/malnutrition)
Acute-on-chronic heart failure leading to hypoperfusion and Type A lactic acidosis
next_steps: Obtain arterial blood gas with serum lactate and anion gap calculation
Perform serum electrolyte panel including bicarbonate and anion gap determination
Obtain arterial or venous blood gas with serum pH and pCO2
Perform blood/urine cultures and search for source of infection given hypotension
Check serum creatinine and renal function profile to assess clearance of metformin
Review medication list and discontinue dapagliflozin immediately
Consult nephrology for consideration of renal replacement therapy if severe acidosis or refractory metabolic failure is present
